sentences: What if "open source" was actually a human shield?
Meta is giving away its AI models for free, calling it the Linux of AI.
The surface story is one of pure benevolence.
But the real mechanism is regulatory capture.
By wrapping their product in the open-source flag, Meta recruits you as an unwitting lobbyist.
It is politically toxic to regulate a community, so they use your work to shield their model.
And when those models are misused, Meta points to a usage policy and walks away.
They build the engine, but you hold all the legal liability.
They aren't just giving you tools; they are externalizing their risk.
background_prompt: A hidden jungle clearing at golden hour, a small limestone pool fed by a gentle, moss-covered waterfall. Soft light filters through the canopy, illuminating floating dust motes and deep green ferns. The scene is intimate and inviting. The lower third of the frame is a dark, calm water surface, keeping the composition simple for text overlay.
